properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for King River.

Minor flooding is likely along the King River. 

Rainfall across the Ovens and King River catchments since Saturday afternoon has resulted in renewed river level rises across the catchments. Further moderate rainfall is forecast until late Tuesday. Minor flooding may develop along the King River at Docker Road Bridge from Sunday evening.

The King River at Docker Rd Bridge is currently at 2.59 m and rising, below the minor flood level. The King River at Docker Rd Bridge may exceed the minor flood level (3.70 m) Sunday evening. Further rises arepossible with forecast rain.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings. 
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 13/07/2026 12:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

Rainfall across the Ovens and King River catchments since Saturday afternoon has resulted in renewed river level rises across the catchments. Further moderate rainfall is forecast until late Tuesday. Minor flooding may develop along the King River at Docker Road Bridge from Sunday evening.

The King River at Docker Rd Bridge is currently at 2.59 m and rising, below the minor flood level. The King River at Docker Rd Bridge may exceed the minor flood level (3.70 m) Sunday evening. Further rises arepossible with forecast rain. 

Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.

properties.name: Advice
properties.location: Kiewa River Downstream of Mongans Bridge
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for Kiewa River Downstream of Mongans Bridge.

Minor flooding is continuing at Bandiana and possible at Kiewa.

The Kiewa River Channel at Kiewa is currently at 2.89 m and steady, below the minor flood level. The Kiewa River Channel at Kiewa may exceed the minor flood level (3.30 m) from Sunday evening. Further river level rises are possible with forecast rainfall. 

The Kiewa River at Bandiana is currently at 2.89 m and steady, with minor flooding. The Kiewa River at Bandiana is expected to remain above the minor flood level (2.80 m) over the next few days.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 13/07/2026 12:30 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for Murray River at Barham.

Flood Warning Number: 2

MINOR FLOODING POSSIBLE AT BARHAM FROM OVERNIGHT SUNDAY INTO MONDAY    

Upstream flows from the Murray River combined with floodwaters from the Goulburn River may cause minor flooding along the Murray River at Barham from overnight Sunday into Monday.

Upstream of Barham, peaks below the minor flood level are expected along the Murray River at Echuca and Torrumbarry over the weekend.

Murray River downstream of Tocumwal to Barham: 
Minor flooding is possible at Barham.

The Murray River at Barham is currently at 5.30 m and steady, below the minor flood level. 
The Murray River at Barham may peak near the minor flood level (5.50 m) overnight Sunday into Monday.

Latest River Heights:
Murray River at Torrumbarry Weir - 6.22 m - Steady, 11:00 am Sat 11/07/26 
Murray River at Barham - 5.30 m - Steady, 11:00 am Sat 11/07/26 
Murray River at Swan Hill -3.18 m - Steady, 11:00 am Sat 11/07/26
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.

Safety Advice: 
  • Don't drive, walk, swim or play in floodwater because it is dangerous. 
  • Stay away from flooded drains, rivers, streams and waterways. 
  • Obey road closure signs. Plan ahead so you don't drive on flooded roads. 
  • Check the ABC and local media for updates. The situation can change quickly, so stay informed.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
  • Campers along the River should consider relocation.
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
  • Boating in floodwaters is dangerous due to debris, submerged trees and other hidden dangers. 

This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 20/07/2026 01:30 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.
